mechanism_narrative: The month-over-month change in the federal funds rate acts as a persistent discount-rate shock; increases in FEDFUNDS raise the cost of capital and reduce equity present values, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A positive rank correlation between FEDFUNDS monthly changes and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a structural threshold, wage pressure builds and compresses profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increase in industrial production and decrease in unemployment captures a broad-based real activity surge that single-series signals miss, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases and UNRATE decreases in the same month
failure_condition: In months where both INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, strong retail sales growth signals overheating consumer demand that accelerates expected Fed policy restraint, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles identified by rising FEDFUNDS, positive RSAFS growth coincides with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below the 750 thousand unit level signals weakening residential investment and forward demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 750, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls act as a real-time proxy for aggregate income and consumption growth; rising PAYEMS drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals a manufacturing recession that precedes broader earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is below its 12-month moving average, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP above 2.0% annualized signals persistent demand momentum that lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2.0% annualized coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances.
